articles.title: I'm Sick of AI Everything
articles.summary: A while back, I stopped using Facebook because I just couldn't take it anymore. Just totally sick of it. I'm honestly getting there with AI. At this point, I would prefer to have anything AI related just be blocked at the browser level. Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47857461 Points: 99 # Comments: 37

articles.title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
articles.summary: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the same companies now competing directly with Cursor for the developer market.

articles.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
articles.summary: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

articles.title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
articles.summary: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.

articles.title: AI backlash is coming for elections
articles.summary: Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning violence. But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused […]

articles.title: GRAI believes AI can make music more social, not replace artists
articles.summary: AI music startup GRAI says fans want to remix tracks, not generate songs from scratch.

articles.title: John Ternus’ first big problem is AI
articles.summary: Less than a year ago, Apple made headlines for a lack of AI announcements at its annual WWDC event. Ten months later, the company has announced that hardware executive John Ternus will succeed longtime CEO Tim Cook as chief executive - and the official release doesn't mention AI once. Ternus, currently Apple's SVP of hardware […]

articles.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in APAC countries
articles.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all of these countries except Japan.

articles.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
articles.summary: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

articles.title: Fortnite developers can make AI characters now — just don’t try to date them
articles.summary: Following last year's AI-powered Darth Vader in Fortnite that swore in a re-creation of James Earl Jones' voice, Epic Games is now letting Fortnite creators experiment with a new "conversations" tool to create AI-powered characters that players can talk and interact with. "Instead of authoring dialogue trees for characters in your islands, conversations transforms an […]

articles.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This start-up wants to figure out which ones matter.
articles.summary: 10x Science has raised a $4.8 million seed round to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules.

articles.title: The most interesting startups showcased at Google Cloud Next 2026
articles.summary: Google wants AI startups on its cloud and has showcased a long list of them at its annual conference.

articles.title: Google Maps is about to get a big dose of AI
articles.summary: Generative AI is being infused into Google's popular feature within Maps.

articles.title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
articles.summary: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

articles.title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
articles.summary: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

articles.title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
articles.summary: This week, during a podcast appearance, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called out his competitor's new cybersecurity model, noting that the company was using fear to make its product sound more impressive than it actually is.

articles.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
articles.summary: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.
